name: CEL SCI CORP
business: CEL-SCI Corporation is a late clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing immunotherapies, primarily Multikine for head and neck cancer and LEAPS technology for rheumatoid arthritis.

exposures.sentence: Multikine, an investigational Phase 3 immunotherapy under development for the potential treatment of certain head and neck cancers.
L.E.A.P.S. (Ligand Epitope Antigen Presentation System) technology, or LEAPS, with several product candidates under development for the potential treatment of rheumatoid arthritis.

catalysts.event: FDA clearance for confirmatory registration study of Multikine
catalysts.sentence: In May 2024, CEL-SCI announced that the FDA indicated CEL-SCI may move forward with a confirmatory registration study of Multikine in the target population.

catalysts.event: Memorandum of Understanding with Dallah Pharma for commercialization in Saudi Arabia
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, CEL-SCI signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Dallah Pharma (Dallah), a subsidiary of Dallah Healthcare Company, for the commercialization of Multikine in Saudi Arabia.

catalysts.event: Breakthrough Medicine Designation application filed with Saudi FDA by Dallah Pharma
catalysts.sentence: In August 2025, CEL-SCI filed a Breakthrough Medicine Designation application with the Saudi Food and Drug Authority (SFDA) for Multikine in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia by one of the Kingdom's premier pharmaceutical and healthcare companies, Dallah Pharma.

catalysts.event: Begin confirmatory registration study of Multikine
catalysts.sentence: CEL-SCI's goal is to begin the 212-patient confirmatory registration study as soon as the needed capital has been raised, with full enrollment about 15 months later with the potential to seek early approval after full enrollment.

tamClaims.claim: Global cancer immunotherapy market expected to reach $196.45 billion by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: The global cancer immunotherapy market is expected to reach USD $196.45 billion by 2030, registering CAGR of 7.2% during the forecast period, according to a new report by Grand View Research, Inc.

tamClaims.claim: Target population of approximately 100,000 patients worldwide per year
tamClaims.sentence: the target population, which accounts for approximately 100,000 patients worldwide per year.
